source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

event.top_event: Employee struck by suspended casing joint
actions_taken: All rig crane operations were stopped
Rig wide safety stand down was performed
IP was evaluated by rig medic and sent onshore to hospital
Transocean redesigned dunnage used on conveyer belt system
Chevron and Transocean began reviewing all WRAs involving tubular handling operations
Transocean implemented new lifting requirements in Health and Safety Manual
event.summary: On August 7, 2016, while running casing on Chevron's PN010 well in Green Canyon 640, a Roustabout was struck in the torso by a 16-inch casing joint that shifted during repositioning of dunnage on the rig's forward conveyor system. The employee was forced over a 42-inch handrail and landed on a riser yoke spreader bar. The Banksman pulled the injured person back to safety. The incident occurred because crew failed to recognize hazards when repositioning dunnage in close proximity to a suspended load, and personnel were not removed from the area before lifting operations continued.
recommendations: Redesign dunnage to prevent separation (four pieces of conveyer belt bolted together)
Review all Work Risk Assessments involving tubular handling operations
Implement additional control measures for tubular lifting operations in Health and Safety Manual
Ensure personnel are removed from conveyor system area before lifting operations begin

hazards.name: Personnel in lifting operation exclusion zone

threats.name: Dunnage knocked off conveyor belt during casing placement
Casing joint shifts when lowered onto aft dunnage
Load swings into personnel in proximity to suspended load

controls.name: Work Risk Assessment (WRA) for casing lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and control measures before work begins

supporting_text: the Work Risk Assessment (WRA) performed by the crew prior to the casing lifting operation failed to cover the repositioning of the dunnage on the conveyer system

controls.name: Personnel exclusion zone during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Keep personnel out of area where suspended load could swing

supporting_text: Normally, rig personnel are not allowed within the area that the incident took place once lifting operations have begun
Failure to remove all personnel from the conveyer system area before proceeding with casing lifting operations

controls.name: Dunnage design for casing conveyor system
controls.barrier_role: Prevent dunnage from separating during casing placement

supporting_text: the top piece of dunnage was knocked off by the casing joint being set down
Failure to recognize that the dunnage design used was inadequate for the application

Transocean has redesigned the dunnage used on their conveyer belt system for casing lifting operations. The new dunnage design consists of four pieces of conveyer belt bolted together which prevents them from separating

controls.name: Banksman communication and signaling
controls.barrier_role: Direct crane operator and coordinate safe load positioning

supporting_text: The Banksman signaled the Crane Operator to pick up on the load in order to reposition the dunnage
The Banksman also recognized the casing's movement and also ducked down
The Banksman immediately pulled Roustabout #1 (IP) back to safety onto the conveyer walkway

controls.name: Situational awareness and hazard recognition by crew
controls.barrier_role: Recognize load shifting and take evasive action

supporting_text: Roustabout #2, standing forward of the conveyer track, noticed the load shifting and proceeded to duck
The Banksman also recognized the casing's movement and also ducked down
Lack of Awareness: The rig crew's failure to notice warning signs as well as identify possible hazards associated with lifting operations

controls.name: Medical response and evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care and transport to hospital

supporting_text: The IP was evaluated by the rig medic and later sent onshore to a hospital for further evaluation

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
